title: Merry Christmas And Happy New Year!
content:
آ آ آ آ Little cullud Rastus come a-skippinï؟½ down de street,
آ آ آ آ A-smilinï؟½ and a-grinninï؟½ at every one he meet;
آ آ آ آ My, oh! He was happy! Boy, but was he gay!
آ آ آ آ Wishinï؟½ ï؟½Merry Chrisï؟½musï؟½ anï؟½ ï؟½Happy New-Yearï؟½s Dayï؟½!
آ آ آ آ Wishinï؟½ that his wishes might every one come true,
آ آ آ آ And, bless your dear heart, honey, I wish the same to you!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Ellis Parker Butler's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
